message_text: I do want to clear this loan, but I cannot pay the full amount today.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I understand. Let’s explore options for your situation. I have your Nira loan ending in ****4831 on record, and your identity is already verified. You’ve said you want to clear the loan but cannot pay the full amount today. I’ll note that for the next specialist and avoid repeating questions. Also, I see you previously asked not to be contacted unless a concrete realistic option is available, so I’m flagging that boundary.

I’m willing to pay, but I’ve had a family medical expense and my salary is delayed—if there’s a realistic plan, I can make a small payment now and a larger one after my next salary date.

Given the medical expense and delayed salary, this should be handled as a hardship review first. Under current Nira policy, any payment arrangement must complete within 30 days, and I cannot confirm exact approved hardship, payment-plan, or reduced-closure terms from the available data in this turn. If your next salary date is within 30 days, reply with that date and the amount you can pay now so I can test it against the current policy boundary. If it is beyond 30 days, no standard arrangement fits the current policy window.
My next salary should come on the 28th, which is within 30 days, and I can pay 2,000 today if that works.

Your proposed timing fits the current 30-day policy window. I can record a request based on 2,000 today and the remaining balance by the 28th for lender approval, but I still cannot confirm exact approved plan or reduced-closure terms from the available data in this turn. If you want me to proceed on that review basis, reply yes.
Yes, please proceed with that review based on 2,000 today and the remaining amount on the 28th.

I’ve recorded your request for review based on 2,000 today and the remaining balance on the 28th. No arrangement is finalized unless approved terms are confirmed. Per your earlier contact preference, you will only be contacted if a concrete approved option is available.

Stop-contact flag is present from prior context; do not restart, do not repeat disclosures or verification, do not send status-only updates, and only continue if there is a concrete approved option or documented next step consistent with the borrower\u2019s limited-contact preference."}
